The paper analyzes the employment response of industries in Puerto Rico to hurricanes, indicating heterogeneity (strengthened, weakened, or neutral responses), but precise percentage change values are not reported on this page. I've entered a placeholder value of 0.0, but note it's not directly from the text.
Construction employment rises sharply within one year of a hurricane, reaching levels about 1.2% higher than in the absence of disaster.
